name: HMS Uther (P 62)
events: The page of this submarine was last updated in March 2018.
HMS Uther (Lt. R.A.A.C. Ward, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
9 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) departed her builders yard for Blyth where she arrived later the same day. (2)
10 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted trials off Blyth. (2)

11 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) departed Blyth for Holy Loch. She made the passage together with HMS Una (Lt. W.G. Meeke, DSC, RN) and HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR). They were escorted by HMS ML 300 (T/Lt. F.C.R. Piesse, RNVR) until 0635/12 when HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR) took over the escort. (2)
14 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) arrived at Holy Loch to begin a period of trials and training.
It soon became apparent that Uther had serious defects to her conning tower casting. These could not be made good in under three months. Uther was therefore limited to A/S training duties. (2)
15 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises in Gare Loch. (2)
18 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (2)
19 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (2)
21 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) shifted from Holy Loch to Arrochar. (2)
22 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (2)
23 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (2)
24 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) shifted from Arrochar to Holy Loch. (2)
25 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. These were followed by exercises. (2)
26 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises and trials off Fairlie. (2)
27 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted speed trials in lower Loch Long. (2)
29 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) shifted from Holy Loch to Campbeltown. Off Campbeltown she conducted exercises with HMS Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.D. O'Driscoll, RNR). (2)
30 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Campbeltown. Upon completion of these exercises HMS Uther proceeded to Holy Loch. (2)
31 Aug 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included night exercises with HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (2)
1 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) returned to Holy Loch upon completion of the exercises. (3)

4 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS White Bear and HMS La Flore (Lt. F.W. Hayden, RN). These were followed by gunnery exercises on a target that was towed by HMS Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.D. O'Driscoll, RNR). (3)
6 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN). (3)

8 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) departed Holy Loch for Scapa Flow. She made the passage together with HMS Stonehenge (Lt. D.S.M. Verschoyle-Campbell, DSC and Bar, RN) that was to proceed to Lerwick. They were escorted north through the Minches by HMS White Bear. (3)
10 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow where she was to participate in A/S exercises. (3)
11 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow. (3)
13 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. J.C. Hibbard, DSC, RCN) and USS Bell (T/Capt. L.C. Peatross, USN).
HMCS Iroquois had already conducted speed trials on the measured mile before joining the A/S exercises. (4)
15 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Hussar (Lt.Cdr. R.C. Biggs, DSO, DSC, RN) and HMS Musketeer (Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN). (3)
16 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Amazon (Lt.Cdr. G. Blackler, RN). (3)
17 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMIS Kistna (A/Cdr.(Emgy) R.R. Caws, RIN) and HMS Oribi (Lt.Cdr. J.C.A. Ingram, DSC and Bar, RN). (3)

18 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Hussar (Lt.Cdr. R.C. Biggs, DSO, DSC, RN), HMIS Kistna (A/Cdr.(Emgy.) R.R. Caws, RIN) and HNoMS Stord (Lt.Cdr. S.V. Storheill). (3)

20 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Vigilant (Lt.Cdr. L.W.L. Argles, RN), HMIS Kistna (A/Cdr.(Emgy.) R.R. Caws, RIN) and HMS Oribi (Lt.Cdr. J.C.A. Ingram, DSC and Bar, RN). (3)
21 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HNoMS Stord (Lt.Cdr. S.V. Storheill). (3)

22 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Jamaica (Capt. J.L. Storey, DSO, RN), HMS Onslow (Capt. J.A. McCoy, DSO, RN) and HMS Impulsive (Lt.Cdr. P. Bekenn, RN). (5)
23 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Impulsive (Lt.Cdr. P. Bekenn, RN).

Upon completion of the exercises Uther departed Scapa Flow for Dundee. She made the passage together with HMS Unbroken (Lt. B.J.B. Andrew, DSC, RN). They were escorted by HMS Scalby Wyke (A/Skr.Lt. C.A. Grimmer, RNR). (3)
24 Sep 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) arrived at Dundee where she joined the 9th submarine flotilla. (3)
4 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Dundee with aircraft. (6)
5 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dundee with HMS Wolsey (Lt.Cdr. S.A. Cuthbert, RN) and HMS Leeds (Lt. B.H. Brown, RN). (6)
6 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dundee with HMS Kingston Onyx (T/Lt. W.C. Morgans, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Ch.Skr. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (6)

7 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dundee with HMS Wells (Lt. D.D. Bone, RN), HMS St. Marys (Lt. D.B.G. Dumas, RN) and HMS Stella Conopus (Skr.Lt. W.J.V. Mullender, DSC, RD, RNR). (6)
8 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Dundee. (6)
9 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) shifted to Rosyth. (6)
11 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) shifted from Rosyth to Dundee. (6)

18 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN), HrMs K XIV (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Drijfhout Van Hooff, RNN) and HMS H 43 (Lt. C.A.J. Nicoll, RN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. They were escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (6)
19 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow where she was to participate in A/S exercises. (6)
20 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Scapa Flow with HMS Urchin (Lt.Cdr. J.T.B. Birch, DSO, DSC, RN) and HMS Ophelia (T/Lt. D.J. Duncan, RNVR). (6)

21 Oct 1943 In the morning, HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN), conducted exercises off Scapa Flow with HMS Queen Elizabeth (Capt. H.G. Norman, CBE, RN) that was escorted by the destroyers HMS Urchin (Lt.Cdr. J.T.B. Birch, DSO, DSC, RN) and USS Capps (T/Cdr. B.E.S. Trippensee, USN).
In the afternoon HMS Queen Elizabeth, with her destroyer escort, conducted range and inclination exercises with HMS Birmingham (Capt. H.W. Williams, RN). (7)

23 Oct 1943 HMS Renown (Capt. W.E. Parry, CB, RN), HMS Birmingham (Capt. H.W. Williams, RN), HMS Ashanti (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Barnes, RN) and USS Forrest (T/Cdr. K.P. Letts, USN, with COMDESDIV 20, T/Capt. C.M. Jensen, USN on board) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow.
In the afternoon HMS Renown and the two destroyers served as targets for the British submarine HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN). (6)
24 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Tuna (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN). (6)
24 Oct 1943 HMS Tuna (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) conducted practice attacks on HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN). (8)
25 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow together with HMS Birmingham (Capt. H.W. Williams, RN) and HMS Jamaica (Capt. J.L. Storey, DSO, RN).
On completion of these A/S exercises, HMS Birmingham and HMS Jamaica conducted gunnery exercises at Scapa Flow.
Before the A/S exercises commenced HMS Jamaica had already carried out a torpedo firing exercise. (9)
27 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow. (6)
28 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Ariadne (Capt. Lord Ashbourne, RN) and HMNZS Gambia (Capt. N.J.W. William-Powlett, DSC, RN).
HMNZS Gambia also carried out gunnery exercises. (10)
29 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow with HMS Ariadne (Capt. Lord Ashbourne, RN) and HMS Ashanti (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Barnes, RN). (6)

31 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) and HMS Tuna (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) both departed Scapa Flow for Blyth. At sea they were joind by HMS Seadog (Lt. C.R. Pelly, RN) and HMS Tantivy (Cdr. M.G. Rimington, DSO, RN) that were en-rout to Dundee and Sheerness respectively. They were escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (6)
2 Nov 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) arrived at Blyth. (11)
6 Nov 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) returned to her builders yard at Walker to make good defects to her conning tower. (11)
9 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) shifted from Walker to Blyth. (12)
10 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) was docked at Blyth. (12)
12 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) was undocked. (12)

17 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) departed Blyth for Rothesay. She makes the passage together with HMS Trusty (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) and HMS Ursula (Lt. A.G. Davies, RN). They were escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR) until 1305/18 when HMS Sardonyx (T/A/Lt.Cdr. E. Playne, RNVR) took over the escort. Also HMS Telemachus (Cdr. W.D.A. King, DSO, DSC, RN) joined the convoy. (12)
21 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) arrived at Rothesay. HMS Uther was now attached to the 7th submarine flotilla for training duties. (12)
29 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted independent exercises in the Clyde area. (12)
31 Dec 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) departed Rothesay for Fishguard. (12)
1 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) arrived at Fishguard. (13)
4 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
5 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
6 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
7 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted gunnery exercises off Fishguard. (13)
8 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
10 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
13 Jan 1944 During the night of 13/14 January 1944, HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
14 Jan 1944 During the night of 14/15 January 1944, HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
16 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
19 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
21 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) was to conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft but no aircraft showed up. (13)
23 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) briefly conducted exercises off Fishguard but these were soon abandoned due to the bad weather conditions. (13)
26 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted indendent exercises off Fishguard. (13)
28 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
29 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
30 Jan 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (13)
7 Feb 1944 During the night of 7/8 February 1944, HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with aircraft. (14)
9 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) departed Fishguard for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Blade (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR). (14)
10 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (14)
14 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) was docked at Kames Bay. (14)
15 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) was undocked. (14)

15 Feb 1944 HrMs O 9 (Lt. J.B.M.J. Maas, RNN) departed Rothesay for Londonderry. She made the passage together with HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN). They were escorted by HMS Kihna (A/Cdr.(Retd.) A.R.W. Sayle, RD, RNR). (15)
18 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (14)
20 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (14)
21 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (14)

22 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR), HMS Wye (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Powys-Smith, RNR), HMS Swale (Lt.Cdr. J. Jackson, DSC, RNR) and HMS Pennywort (T/Lt. A.W. James, RNR). (14)

23 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR), HMS Wye (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Powys-Smith, RNR), HMS Gorleston (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.A.E. Leonard, RNZNVR) and HMS Pennywort (T/Lt. A.W. James, RNR). (14)
24 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) shifted from Tobermory to Oban. She was escorted by HMS ML 453 (T/Lt. L.V. Horne, RNVR). (14)
25 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) shifted from Oban to Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS ML 453 (T/Lt. L.V. Horne, RNVR). (14)

26 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Gorleston (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.A.E. Leonard, RNZNVR), HMS Quadrille (T/Lt. G.A. Peat, RNVR) and HMS Kilbride (Lt. H.O. L’Estrange, DSC, RNR). (14)

27 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR), HMS Pennywort (T/Lt. A.W. James, RNR), HMS Quadrille (T/Lt. G.A. Peat, RNVR) and HMS Kilbride (Lt. H.O. L’Estrange, DSC, RNR). (14)

28 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Gorleston (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.A.E. Leonard, RNZNVR), HMS Pennywort (T/Lt. A.W. James, RNR), HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR) and HMS Wye (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Powys-Smith, RNR). (14)
29 Feb 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR) and HMS Wye (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Powys-Smith, RNR). (14)

2 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Lightfoot (Lt.Cdr. T.M. Williams, RNR), HMCS Qa'Appelle (Cdr. D.C. Wallace, DSC, RCNR) and HMS Wye (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Powys-Smith, RNR). (16)

3 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMCS Qa'Appelle (Cdr. D.C. Wallace, DSC, RCNR), HMS Lightfoot (Lt.Cdr. T.M. Williams, RNR) and HMS Longa (T/Lt. H.E. Smith, SANF(V)). (16)

4 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR), HMS Longa (T/Lt. H.E. Smith, SANF(V)) and HMCS Qa'Appelle (Cdr. D.C. Wallace, DSC, RCNR). (16)

6 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR), HMS Rushen Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Warwick, DSC, RNR), HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J.P. Roberts, RNR) and HMCS Qa'Appelle (Cdr. D.C. Wallace, DSC, RCNR). (16)

7 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Kingsmill (Lt. G.H. Cook, RN), HMS Nadder (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.E. Kitto, RNR), HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR) and HMS Rushen Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Warwick, DSC, RNR). (16)

8 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J.P. Roberts, RNR), HMS Nadder (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.E. Kitto, RNR), HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR) and HMS Rushen Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Warwick, DSC, RNR). (16)
9 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J.P. Roberts, RNR) and HMS Nadder (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.E. Kitto, RNR). (16)

13 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with FFS L'Escarmouche, HMS Rushen Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Warwick, DSC, RNR), HMS Nadder (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.E. Kitto, RNR) and HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR). (16)

14 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR) and HMS Rushen Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Warwick, DSC, RNR), HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J.P. Roberts, RNR) and HMS Nadder (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.E. Kitto, RNR). (16)

15 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Leeds Castle (A/Lt.Cdr. J. Mackay, RNR), HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J.P. Roberts, RNR), HMS Dovey (T/A/Lt.Cdr. T.W.F. Bolland, RNR) and HMS Loring (Lt. A.d'E.T. Sangster, RN). (16)
21 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with FFS L'Escarmouche and FFS L'Aventure. (16)

22 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with FFS L'Escarmouche, FFS L'Aventure, HMS Dovey (T/A/Lt.Cdr. T.W.F. Bolland, RNR) and HMS Loring (Lt. A.d'E.T. Sangster, RN). (16)

23 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Leeds Castle (A/Lt.Cdr. J. Mackay, RNR), HMS Oxford Castle (T/Lt. H.E. Holden, DSC, RNR), HMS Dovey (T/A/Lt.Cdr. T.W.F. Bolland, RNR) and HMS Loring (Lt. A.d'E.T. Sangster, RN). (16)
24 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Leeds Castle (A/Lt.Cdr. J. Mackay, RNR) and HMS Oxford Castle (T/Lt. H.E. Holden, DSC, RNR). (16)
27 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Hoste (Lt. P.J.H. Hoare, RN) and HMS Inglis (Lt. R.S. Beveridge, RN). (16)

28 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Moorsom (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Stewart, DSC, RD, RNR), HMS Seymour (Lt. G.J. Parry, RNR) and HMS Stayner (Lt.Cdr. H.J. Hall, DSO, DSC, RNR). (16)

29 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Seymour (Lt. G.J. Parry, RNR), HMS Stayner (Lt.Cdr. H.J. Hall, DSO, DSC, RNR), HMS Rowley (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Jones, RD, RNR) and HMS Retalick (Lt. J.S. Brownrigg, RN). (16)
31 Mar 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) shifted from Tobermory to Stornoway. She was escorted by HMS Clorinde (T/Lt. M.L. Patterson, RNVR). (16)
1 Apr 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway with HMCS Hespeler (T/Lt.Cdr. N.S.C. Dickinson, RCNVR) and HMS Man o' War (T/Lt. P.W. Arnold, RNVR). (17)
3 Apr 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway with HMS Lord Austin (T/Lt. E.S.T. Robinson, RNVR) and HMS Southern Shore (Skr. S. Hearne, RNR). (17)
5 Apr 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Stornoway. (17)
7 Apr 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Stornoway. (17)
11 Apr 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) departed Stornoway for Campbeltown. She was escorted by HMS Kihna (Cdr.(Retd.) T.J.T.C. Jenks, RN). (17)
12 Apr 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) arrived at Campbeltown. (17)

8 Jun 1944 HMS Uther (Lt. A.A. Catlow, RN) proceeded from Rothesay to Kames Bay where she was immediately docked. (1)

[No logs are available for November and December 1944, therefore no details can be given for the operations of HMS Uther other then the ones listed here.] (25)

7 Apr 1945 HMS Uther (Lt. R.A.A.C. Ward, DSC, RN) and HMS Urtica (Lt. R.C. Bucknall, RN) departed Rothesay for Plymouth and Holyhead respectively. They were escorted by HMS Alecto (Cdr.(Retd.) E.S. Felton, RN). (11)
10 Apr 1945 HMS Uther (Lt. R.A.A.C. Ward, DSC, RN) arrived at Plymouth. (11)
11 Apr 1945 HMS Uther (Lt. R.A.A.C. Ward, DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for Tilbury. (11)
14 Apr 1945 HMS Uther (Lt. R.A.A.C. Ward, DSC, RN) arrived at Tilbury where she was to refit.
When the Second World War ended Uther was still refitting. The refit was completed in October.
Uther was paid off into reserve on 15 November 1945. (11)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
